achievements: Provider abstraction overhaul: generic control descriptors, providerOptions buckets, runtime metadata, adapter-owned snapshots/capabilities, ProviderRuntimeEvent envelopes, and conformance fixtures across Claude/Codex/Cursor
Fixed multiple persistence regressions: row-level provider metadata writes, synchronous transcript saves on Codex/Cursor, quota fallback chain, and Codex stale resume id recovery
Cursor delegation child MCP wiring: child turns now write real T64 delegation env into .cursor/mcp.json with auto-approval
Removed party-mode (frontend + backend + audio deps) and reorganized Settings into provider-owned cards
Added focus mode for provider chats (X keybind) with screen-centered geometry, inverse-zoom counter-scaling, 65vw width, and snap geometry to avoid scale tween artifacts
Skill integration: bounded skill catalog + matched SKILL.md bodies injected into prompts; system-reminder blocks stripped from visible chat/history
Tightened prompt rewriter to Sonnet high-effort with concise structured guidance and source_prompt boundaries
External link handler: chat markdown links now open in OS default browser instead of inside the Tauri WebView
Roblox toolchain PATH expansion (Aftman/Rokit/Foreman/Wally) so luau-lsp resolves under provider CLIs
MCP config insertion now merges instead of clobbering unrelated provider MCP entries
improvements: Drop legacy compatibility mirrors (selectedModel/selectedEffort/selectedCodexPermission/codexThreadId) once all call sites are migrated — currently kept as shims, ripe for removal
Frontend providerSnapshots still has static manifest fallback duplicating backend descriptors; collapse to single backend-owned source like t3code
Hydrate server PATH from login shell/launchctl/PowerShell at startup (per t3code research) instead of only patching at provider spawn
Per-provider binary/env override settings (t3code parity) — currently providers use a single shared resolver
Vite build still emits chunk-size and dynamic-import warnings; address with manualChunks and consistent import strategy
Consolidate the many delegation team-chat .md files at repo root into a subdirectory or .gitignore them — they pollute the root
Replace deprecated ProviderTurnInput legacy field reads with explicit selectedControls/runtimeMetadata-only paths (TypeScript can enforce)
next_tasks: Remove ProviderTurnInput legacy fields and the selectedModel/selectedEffort compatibility mirrors; rely on selectedControls/providerOptions exclusively
Make providerSnapshots fully backend-owned (drop frontend static fallback) and add a snapshot-versioning/cache-invalidation contract
Implement startup PATH hydration (shell/launchctl/PowerShell) and per-provider binary/env settings UI
Long-path manifest for Windows (was deferred during Windows compat pass) — revisit now that tauri-build behavior is understood
Add CI job to run cargo test providers:: and the providerConformance/providerModularity verification fixtures so regressions surface before merge
Move root-level terminal64-delegation-*.md logs into a dedicated directory (e.g. .terminal64-logs/) and gitignore
Sign macOS/Windows builds (Apple notarytool + Azure Trusted Signing) — release workflow ships unsigned today
Audit remaining hardcoded model/effort references in delegation child runtime and provider scaffold script
risks: Compatibility shims (selectedCodexPermission, codexThreadId, selectedModel/effort) are still read in fallback paths — silent regressions possible if a new provider forgets to populate the canonical fields
Provider metadata persistence has 4-tier quota fallback (full → compact → active-only → no-transcript); complex code path with high test surface, fragile under localStorage pressure
Frontend snapshot static fallback can drift from backend descriptors — already observed during provider-control schema migrations
voice/whisper.rs and useVoiceControl.ts marked load-bearing in CLAUDE.md but have grown complex (windowed decode, AgreementBuffer, jarvis word-eat) — regression risk on any voice edit
Unsigned binaries: macOS gatekeeper friction for end users until Apple notarytool secrets are added

Widget HTTP server, permission server, PTY spawn, and Discord bot are the exposed surfaces per SECURITY.md — recent edits to permission server and Discord session tracking need a focused security review
Delegation team-chat .md files are accumulating at repo root and being committed; potential to leak conversational content from past sessions
buglog.json now has 180+ entries — without periodic consolidation, future sessions waste tokens scanning it before fixes
